claim: Lower-right rows provide section-effective pressure residual evidence
required_wording: Allowed: lower-right two-tap rows are section-effective diagnostics showing hydrostatic dominance and ordinary-basis failure.

claim: Negative residuals support the need for hybrid pressure modeling
required_wording: Allowed: negative signed residuals are evidence that throughflow-plus-recirculation or section-effective modeling is needed before component K/F6 extraction.

claim: Component K is admitted from lower-right rows
required_wording: Forbidden: component isolation, ordinary-flow, and same-QOI UQ gates fail.
Forbidden: ordinary candidate pairs, same-QOI UQ, endpoint source fields, and source/property release rows are zero.

claim: F6 is numerically better or worse than F3/Shah
required_wording: Forbidden: F3/F6 comparison is not evaluated because no F6 ordinary candidate is admitted.

claim: Negative K is a physical loss coefficient
required_wording: Forbidden: negative K is an artifact of basis mismatch and recirculating section behavior, not a component coefficient.

claim: Clipped K or hidden multiplier fixes pressure
required_wording: Forbidden: no clipped K, global multiplier, or residual absorption is admitted.
